ops.title: P5 regression r2: R-I cohort fix surfaces business (J) end-to-end
ops.body:
P5 regression round 2 (trip 2026-09-p5-canonical-r2, shipped through 3856327d, R-I cohort fix IN). Ask verbatim: "warm beachy week in september, business, avoid the usual seoul/tokyo". Plan: outbound WST→[OKA,USM,HNL] bucket "beach" + return $origins; window 2026-09-06..2026-09-20; cabin business; party 1; avoid ICN/GMP/NRT/HND; NO lodging.

Results (all 4 criteria PASS, quota spent 32, no floor stop at --quota-floor 300):
- C1 compile: canonical 8-node two-intent graph (sweep:outbound:beach, shortlist:outbound, sweep:return, shortlist:return, expand, assess, rank, finalize); trip_type round_trip, lodging false, requires [].
- C2 walk: all 8 nodes stamped fresh; expand composed 115 award→award journeys (0 unpaired/gated); finalists 6 journeys + 1 notable stretch, 0 unpaired.
- C3 R-I LIVE: J now surfaces (round 1 was all-economy). Outbound shortlist 4 J rows (LAS/LAX/SAN→HNL Alaska), return 4 J (HNL→LAS/PHX). 16 J→J round trips in expand.json, all preference_misses=[] (no phantom cabin miss). Business option in finalists as the engine-surfaced notable stretch (SAN→PHX→HNL + HNL→LAX→LAS J/J, 70000 mi Alaska, seat_sufficiency sufficient) with why "clears your business cabin preference — every finalist misses it". Top-6 finalists are economy Y/W (correct cost-lane ranking, 25k<70k mi), each carrying its honest double cabin miss.
- C4 provenance: both sweeps completeness=complete; search_states preserved (OKA complete, USM searched_empty, HNL complete); each complete sweep cut one new generation (sweep ids 10, 11); "incomplete pins" is the documented store.py _resolve_sweep counterpart (if not complete: return base_generation), not exercised (no partial endpoint this run).

Nuance: no J→J composed fully nonstop — all 16 route via a PHX/LAX connection because the "direct"-flagged availability ids expand into connecting trip details; direct J rows are present in the shortlists themselves.
